title: लक्ष्मी आरती
titleEn: Lakshmi Aarti - Om Jai Lakshmi Mata
devanagariText:
॥ श्री लक्ष्मी जी की आरती ॥

ॐ जय लक्ष्मी माता, मैया जय लक्ष्मी माता।
तुमको निसदिन सेवत, हरि विष्णु विधाता॥
ॐ जय लक्ष्मी माता॥

उमा रमा ब्रह्माणी, तुम ही जग माता।
सूर्य चन्द्रमा ध्यावत, नारद ऋषि गाता॥
ॐ जय लक्ष्मी माता॥

दुर्गा रूप निरंजनी, सुख सम्पत्ति दाता।
जो कोई तुमको ध्यावत, ऋद्धि सिद्धि धन पाता॥
ॐ जय लक्ष्मी माता॥

तुम पाताल निवासिनी, तुम ही शुभदाता।
कर्म प्रभाव प्रकाशिनी, भवनिधि की त्राता॥
ॐ जय लक्ष्मी माता॥

जिस घर में तुम रहती, सब सद्गुण आता।
सब सम्भव हो जाता, मन नहीं घबराता॥
ॐ जय लक्ष्मी माता॥

तुम बिन यज्ञ न होवे, वस्त्र न कोई पाता।
खान पान का वैभव, सब तुमसे आता॥
ॐ जय लक्ष्मी माता॥

शुभगुण मन्दिर सुन्दर, क्षीरोदधि जाता।
रत्न चतुर्दश तुम बिन, कोई नहीं पाता॥
ॐ जय लक्ष्मी माता॥

महालक्ष्मी जी की आरती, जो कोई जन गाता।
उर आनन्द समाता, पाप उतर जाता॥
ॐ जय लक्ष्मी माता॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Lakshmi Ji Ki Aarti ||

Om Jai Lakshmi Mata, Maiya Jai Lakshmi Mata.
Tumko Nisdin Sevat, Hari Vishnu Vidhata.
Om Jai Lakshmi Mata.

Uma Rama Brahmani, Tum Hi Jag Mata.
Surya Chandrama Dhyavat, Narad Rishi Gata.
Om Jai Lakshmi Mata.

Durga Roop Niranjani, Sukh Sampatti Data.
Jo Koi Tumko Dhyavat, Riddhi Siddhi Dhan Pata.
Om Jai Lakshmi Mata.

Tum Patal Nivasini, Tum Hi Shubhdata.
Karm Prabhav Prakashini, Bhavnidhi Ki Trata.
Om Jai Lakshmi Mata.

Jis Ghar Mein Tum Rehti, Sab Sadgun Aata.
Sab Sambhav Ho Jata, Man Nahin Ghabrata.
Om Jai Lakshmi Mata.

Tum Bin Yagya Na Hove, Vastra Na Koi Pata.
Khan Pan Ka Vaibhav, Sab Tumse Aata.
Om Jai Lakshmi Mata.

Shubhgun Mandir Sundar, Kshirodadhi Jata.
Ratna Chaturdash Tum Bin, Koi Nahin Pata.
Om Jai Lakshmi Mata.

Mahalakshmi Ji Ki Aarti, Jo Koi Jan Gata.
Ur Anand Samata, Paap Utar Jata.
Om Jai Lakshmi Mata.
hindiMeaning:
हे माता लक्ष्मी! आपकी जय हो। हरि विष्णु और ब्रह्मा सदैव आपकी सेवा करते हैं।

आप उमा, रमा और ब्रह्माणी हैं, आप ही जगत की माता हैं। सूर्य और चन्द्रमा आपका ध्यान करते हैं, नारद ऋषि आपके गुण गाते हैं।

आप दुर्गा रूप निरंजनी हैं, सुख और सम्पत्ति देने वाली हैं। जो कोई आपका ध्यान करता है, वह ऋद्धि, सिद्धि और धन प्राप्त करता है।

जिस घर में आप निवास करती हैं, वहाँ सब सद्गुण आते हैं। सब कुछ सम्भव हो जाता है और मन नहीं घबराता।

महालक्ष्मी जी की यह आरती जो कोई गाता है, उसके हृदय में आनन्द समाता है और पाप दूर हो जाते हैं।
benefits: Invokes the blessings of Goddess Lakshmi for household prosperity
Traditionally recited for removal of poverty and brings financial stability
Fills the home with positive energy and good qualities
Invokes the blessing of riddhi (prosperity) and siddhi (spiritual powers)
Traditionally recited for removal of sins and fills the heart with joy
Essential part of Diwali puja and daily evening prayers
description: Dedicated to Goddess Lakshmi, the Lakshmi Aarti - Om Jai Lakshmi Mata (लक्ष्मी आरती) is a traditional worship song (aarti) revered in Hindu tradition. This revered text originates from Traditional. Devotees recite this prayer to remove sins and fills the heart with joy and remove poverty and bring financial stability. It is also said to invoke the blessings of Goddess Lakshmi for household prosperity. For best results, devotees chant this on Friday (Shukravar) and during Diwali. With 8 verses and a reading time of about 5 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Lakshmi Aarti - Om Jai Lakshmi Mata in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Om Jai Lakshmi Mata Aarti in Hindi with English transliteration and meaning. Essential Lakshmi aarti for Diwali puja and daily worship.
relatedSearches: Om Jai Lakshmi Mata lyrics in Hindi
Lakshmi aarti by Pandit Shardha Ram Phillauri
Lakshmi aarti meaning verse by verse

seoFaqs.question: Who composed the 'Om Jai Lakshmi Mata' aarti?
seoFaqs.answer: The 'Om Jai Lakshmi Mata' aarti is widely attributed to Pandit Shraddha Ram Phillauri (1837–1881), a 19th-century scholar, kathavachak, and reformer from Phillaur in Punjab. Phillauri is also credited with composing the universally sung 'Om Jai Jagdish Hare' aarti. He created these aartis as simple, melodious devotional songs in Hindustani (a blend of Hindi and Urdu vocabulary) so that the core tenets of Sanatana Dharma could be sung and understood by common worshippers across regions. (Source: Vedic Stories; Lovenspire.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the Lakshmi Aarti traditionally sung?
seoFaqs.answer: The Lakshmi Aarti is sung at the conclusion of Lakshmi puja, most prominently on Diwali (Lakshmi Pujan night, Amavasya of Kartik), on Sharad Purnima (Kojagiri), during Varalakshmi Vrata in Shravan, and every Friday, the day traditionally associated with Goddess Lakshmi. It is also sung daily during morning and evening puja in many North Indian households, particularly after lighting the diya before the Lakshmi-Ganesh image. (Source: Drik Panchang; My Pooja Box.)

seoFaqs.question: What does the aarti say about Lakshmi's forms?
seoFaqs.answer: The verses of 'Om Jai Lakshmi Mata' praise the Goddess as the consort of Hari Vishnu Vidhata, the mother of the universe, and the bestower of wealth, food, and dharmic prosperity. The aarti names her relationships with Brahma, Vishnu, and Mahesh, calls her the giver of moksha and dharma, and acknowledges her as the source of bliss for her devotees. It is essentially a stuti (praise) that condenses the broader Lakshmi theology of the Puranas into eight short, singable verses. (Source: Vedic Stories; Servdharm.)

seoFaqs.question: How is this aarti related to 'Om Jai Jagdish Hare'?
seoFaqs.answer: Both aartis share the same composer, Pandit Shraddha Ram Phillauri, the same metrical structure, and the same congregational singing pattern with a refrain repeated after each verse. 'Om Jai Jagdish Hare' is addressed to the supreme Vishnu (Jagdish, lord of the world), while 'Om Jai Lakshmi Mata' is addressed to his consort Lakshmi. They are often sung in sequence during Diwali Lakshmi Puja and Satyanarayan Katha, and have become the standard aarti format used across most Vaishnava devotional gatherings in North India. (Source: Bagalamukhi.com; Vedic Stories.)

seoFaqs.question: What rituals accompany the singing of Lakshmi Aarti?
seoFaqs.answer: The aarti is performed with a five-wick (panchadipa) or single-wick ghee lamp circled clockwise before the image of Lakshmi, accompanied by ringing the puja bell, lighting incense, and offering kheer, batashe, kheel, lotus flowers, and red cloth. Devotees stand with folded hands, sing along, and at the close of the aarti receive the flame in their palms and touch their eyes and forehead. Coins, currency, and account books are also offered before Lakshmi during Diwali Pujan. (Source: My Pooja Box; Pandit Ji Onway.)
